Land & Development ● Active Australia

Gatton Land
Subdivision

Strategic subdivision opportunity within a high-growth regional corridor in South East Queensland.

Project Value AUD 180M
Total Lots 267
Site Area 120 ha
Hold Period 4–5 Yr
Status ● Active
Overview

Opportunity Overview

This opportunity involves the subdivision and development of a large-scale land parcel within a key growth corridor in South East Queensland. The project is positioned to benefit from strong population growth, infrastructure investment, and increasing demand for affordable housing in regional markets.

Gatton sits within the Lockyer Valley — one of the fastest-growing regional corridors in Australia — driven by affordability migration from Brisbane and the Gold Coast, infrastructure spending, and expanding employment.

"Early access to this corridor is the opportunity. Supply constraints won't ease — demand will only increase."

Investment Thesis

Why This Opportunity

  • Located within a high-growth corridor driven by migration from major cities — Brisbane, Gold Coast, and Sunshine Coast
  • Strong and sustained demand for affordable housing in regional Queensland as urban affordability deteriorates
  • Limited competing supply of titled land in surrounding areas — first mover advantage
  • Supported by ongoing government infrastructure and regional development initiatives in the Lockyer Valley
  • Clear and de-risked pathway to staged development, titled lot releases, and exit
  • Attractive entry point relative to comparable market transactions
Key Metrics

Project Snapshot

AUD 180M Total Project Value
267 Residential Lots
120 ha Site Area
3–5 Yr Development Timeline
Staged Exit Strategy
Equity Investment Type
Structure

Investment Structure

The opportunity is structured through a combination of equity participation and structured capital solutions, allowing for flexible investor participation aligned with project delivery milestones.

The target hold period is approximately 4–5 years, with returns generated through staged land releases and sales. Capital is deployed progressively against construction and titling milestones.

Both income yield during the hold period and profit participation on exit are available depending on investor structure and appetite.

Market Context

Why South East Queensland Now

South East Queensland continues to experience the strongest population growth of any Australian region — driven by interstate migration, infrastructure investment, and a sustained affordability gap versus Sydney and Melbourne.

Regional hubs such as Gatton are benefiting directly from this expansion. Infrastructure spending in the Lockyer Valley is accelerating, new employment is being created, and demand for residential land has materially outpaced supply for the past three years.

For capital positioned early, this dynamic creates a compelling window — the fundamentals are structural, not cyclical.

Our Role

What A.P.Property Does

1 Sourcing the opportunity — identified through direct relationships with developers and local operators. Access before the market.
2 Structuring capital participation — aligning investor objectives with project structure, returns, and risk profile.
3 Partnering with developers — working alongside experienced delivery teams through planning, construction, and titling.
4 Supporting exit and hold strategy — overseeing staged releases, pricing strategy, and capital return to investors.
